title: Robust LapTime and degradation outlier handling
summary: Clip or transform extreme LapTime, LapTime_Delta, and Cumulative_Degradation values before building ratio and projection features.
rationale: Raw-data inspection showed very large lap-time and delta extremes, and these columns feed several engineered ratios. Robust preprocessing can prevent a few anomalous rows from dominating model splits or derived features.
implementation_hint: Winsorize selected numeric columns using training quantiles inside each fold or globally on training data only, then create clipped absolute and positive versions. Compare clipped-features-plus-raw against raw-only so the model can still use anomaly information if helpful.
expected_effect: Small stability gain with medium confidence; AUC impact may be small but should reduce brittle splits.
risk: Over-clipping can remove genuine safety-car or pit-lap signals. Keep raw columns alongside clipped variants in the first experiment.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
